metadata.description: Golden dataset for NLP pipeline regression testing (CTO-3)

notes.description: Heart failure patient with standard medication list and negated chest pain
notes.text: Assessment: 72-year-old male with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction. Ejection fraction: 35%. Patient denies chest pain and shortness of breath. Currently on carvedilol 25mg twice daily, furosemide 40mg daily, and lisinopril 10mg daily. BNP: 450 pg/mL. Labs show creatinine: 1.4 mg/dL. No evidence of pulmonary edema on chest X-ray.

expected_mentions.text: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
expected_mentions.concept_name: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

notes.description: Type 2 diabetes with lab values and medication adjustments
notes.text: HPI: 58-year-old female with type 2 diabetes mellitus presents for routine follow-up. HbA1c: 7.2%. Fasting glucose: 142 mg/dL. Currently taking metformin 1000mg twice daily and empagliflozin 25mg daily. Creatinine: 0.9 mg/dL. eGFR: 78 mL/min. Patient reports occasional dizziness. No nausea or vomiting. Blood pressure: 132/84 mmHg.

notes.description: Oncology note with negated metastasis and family history
notes.text: Assessment: 65-year-old male with stage IIB non-small cell lung cancer status post right upper lobectomy. PET/CT shows no evidence of metastasis. Family history significant for mother had breast cancer at age 55 and father had coronary artery disease. Currently on immunotherapy. Denies fever, cough, or dyspnea. Hemoglobin: 11.2 g/dL. WBC: 5.4 K/uL. Platelets: 198 K/uL.

notes.description: Hypothetical and conditional statements
notes.text: Plan: If symptoms worsen, consider starting prednisone 40mg daily taper. Risk of developing pneumonia given immunosuppressed state. Screening for depression recommended. Patient should return to clinic in 2 weeks. If fever develops, evaluate for possible sepsis. Currently taking acetaminophen 500mg as needed for headache.

notes.description: Atrial fibrillation with comprehensive medication list
notes.text: Assessment: 68-year-old female with atrial fibrillation, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia. Heart rate: 78 bpm. Blood pressure: 128/76 mmHg. Currently on apixaban 5mg twice daily, metoprolol 50mg daily, amlodipine 5mg daily, and atorvastatin 40mg daily. No edema. Denies palpitations. INR: 2.1. Potassium: 4.2 mmol/L. Creatinine: 1.0 mg/dL.

notes.description: Atopic dermatitis trial-relevant note with negated conditions
notes.text: HPI: 34-year-old female with moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis affecting bilateral upper extremities and trunk. No evidence of cellulitis or urticaria. Denies fever. Previously treated with topical corticosteroids without adequate response. Currently applying fluticasone cream. Considering dupilumab if symptoms persist. Family history of asthma in brother.

notes.description: Diabetic macular edema (DME) trial-relevant note
notes.text: Assessment: 62-year-old male with type 2 diabetes mellitus and diabetic macular edema (DME) of the right eye. Visual acuity 20/60 OD. HbA1c: 8.1%. Currently receiving intravitreal injections. Blood pressure: 140/88 mmHg. Creatinine: 1.2 mg/dL. eGFR: 62 mL/min. No proliferative diabetic retinopathy. Takes metformin 1000mg and insulin glargine 20 units at bedtime.

notes.description: CSCC trial-relevant note with procedure history
notes.text: Assessment: 71-year-old male with locally advanced cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (CSCC) of the left temple. Prior surgical excision with positive margins. No evidence of distant metastasis on imaging. Considering immunotherapy with cemiplimab. Labs: Hemoglobin: 13.1 g/dL, WBC: 7.2 K/uL, Creatinine: 0.8 mg/dL, ALT: 22 U/L. Patient denies fever or weight loss. No history of organ transplant.

notes.description: COPD exacerbation with comprehensive vitals and medications
notes.text: Assessment: 66-year-old female with acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. SpO2: 91% on room air. Respiratory rate: 24/min. Heart rate: 102 bpm. Blood pressure: 148/92 mmHg. Temperature: 38.2 C. Started on prednisone 40mg, azithromycin 500mg, and albuterol nebulizer treatments. Chest X-ray shows no pneumonia. WBC: 11.2 K/uL.

notes.description: Chronic kidney disease with electrolyte panel
notes.text: Assessment: 70-year-old male with chronic kidney disease stage 3B and hypertension. Creatinine: 2.1 mg/dL. eGFR: 32 mL/min. BUN: 38 mg/dL. Potassium: 5.3 mmol/L. Sodium: 138 mmol/L. Bicarbonate: 20 mmol/L. Hemoglobin: 10.8 g/dL. No hyperkalemia symptoms. Denies nausea. Currently on losartan 50mg, furosemide 20mg, and alendronate 70mg weekly.

notes.description: Acute coronary syndrome with multiple negations
notes.text: Assessment: 55-year-old male presents with acute myocardial infarction. Troponin: 2.4 ng/mL. EKG shows ST elevation. No evidence of heart failure. Denies nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea. No prior history of coronary artery disease. Started on aspirin 325mg, clopidogrel 75mg, and heparin drip. Blood pressure: 108/72 mmHg. Heart rate: 92 bpm.

notes.description: Multi-system review with negations in review of systems
notes.text: Review of Systems: Patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, abdominal pain, headache, and fever. Reports fatigue and occasional dizziness. No constipation or diarrhea. No syncope. Past medical history includes hypertension and obesity. Medications: lisinopril 20mg daily, hydrochlorothiazide 25mg daily.

notes.description: Stroke workup with uncertain diagnosis
notes.text: Assessment: 60-year-old female with possible transient ischemic attack versus migraine. CT head negative for hemorrhage. MRI pending. Probable carotid stenosis on ultrasound. Blood pressure: 162/98 mmHg. Heart rate: 76 bpm. Started on aspirin 81mg. Glucose: 110 mg/dL. Suspected atrial fibrillation on telemetry. No seizure activity. NIHSS: 2.

notes.description: Type 1 diabetes with comprehensive lab panel
notes.text: HPI: 28-year-old male with type 1 diabetes mellitus on insulin pump therapy. HbA1c: 6.8%. Fasting glucose: 118 mg/dL. TSH normal. No diabetic ketoacidosis symptoms. Denies nausea, vomiting, or abdominal pain. Takes insulin lispro via pump. Creatinine: 0.7 mg/dL. eGFR: >90 mL/min. LDL: 102 mg/dL. HDL: 55 mg/dL. Triglycerides: 128 mg/dL.

notes.description: Family history section with multiple relatives
notes.text: Family History: Mother had breast cancer diagnosed at age 52 and type 2 diabetes mellitus. Father had coronary artery disease and died of acute myocardial infarction at age 64. Sister has asthma and hypothyroidism. Brother diagnosed with depression. No family history of stroke or epilepsy.

notes.description: Medication reconciliation with brand and generic names
notes.text: Medications: Lipitor (atorvastatin) 40mg daily, Lasix (furosemide) 40mg daily, Coreg (carvedilol) 12.5mg twice daily, Prilosec (omeprazole) 20mg daily, Tylenol (acetaminophen) 500mg as needed, Benadryl (diphenhydramine) 25mg at bedtime. Patient reports taking all medications as prescribed. No adverse drug reactions reported.

notes.description: Heart failure with preserved EF and complex medication list
notes.text: Assessment: 75-year-old female with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction, atrial fibrillation, and chronic kidney disease. Ejection fraction: 55%. BNP: 320 pg/mL. Creatinine: 1.6 mg/dL. eGFR: 38 mL/min. Currently on spironolactone 25mg, metoprolol 100mg, warfarin 5mg, and furosemide 80mg daily. No pulmonary edema. Denies dyspnea at rest. INR: 2.5.

expected_mentions.text: heart failure with preserved ejection fraction
expected_mentions.concept_name: Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction

notes.description: Emergency department note with multiple findings
notes.text: HPI: 45-year-old female presents to ED with chest pain and palpitations. Denies shortness of breath, fever, or syncope. No prior history of heart failure or coronary artery disease. Temperature: 37.1 C. Blood pressure: 118/74 mmHg. Heart rate: 112 bpm. SpO2: 98%. Troponin: <0.01 ng/mL. EKG shows sinus tachycardia. Possible costochondritis. Started on ibuprofen 600mg.

notes.description: Psychiatry consultation with medication list
notes.text: Assessment: 38-year-old male with major depressive disorder and generalized anxiety disorder. Denies suicidal ideation. No psychosis. Reports difficulty sleeping. Currently on sertraline 100mg daily, trazodone 50mg at bedtime, and lorazepam 0.5mg as needed. Considering augmentation with bupropion if no improvement. Family history of depression in mother.

notes.description: Infectious disease with lab values and antibiotic therapy
notes.text: Assessment: 52-year-old male admitted with community-acquired pneumonia and sepsis. Temperature: 39.1 C. Heart rate: 118 bpm. Blood pressure: 92/58 mmHg. SpO2: 89% on room air. WBC: 18.4 K/uL. Lactate: 3.2 mmol/L. Procalcitonin: 4.8 ng/mL. Started on ceftriaxone 2g IV and azithromycin 500mg IV. No pleural effusion on imaging.

notes.description: Autoimmune condition with lab work
notes.text: Assessment: 42-year-old female with suspected systemic lupus erythematosus. Reports fatigue, joint pain, and skin rash. ESR: 48 mm/hr. CRP: 12.5 mg/L. No renal involvement. Creatinine: 0.8 mg/dL. Hemoglobin: 11.5 g/dL. WBC: 3.8 K/uL. Platelet count: 132 K/uL. Started on hydroxychloroquine and prednisone 10mg daily. No evidence of deep vein thrombosis.

notes.description: Discharge summary with medication changes
notes.text: Discharge Diagnosis: Acute decompensated heart failure, atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response, type 2 diabetes mellitus, and chronic kidney disease stage 3A. Hospital course: Patient presented with acute decompensated heart failure. Treated with IV furosemide with good diuresis. BNP improved from 1200 to 450 pg/mL. Ejection fraction: 30%. Discharge medications: carvedilol 25mg, furosemide 60mg, lisinopril 10mg, apixaban 5mg, metformin 500mg. INR: 2.3. Creatinine: 1.5 mg/dL.

notes.description: GI consultation with multiple negations and procedure
notes.text: Assessment: 48-year-old male with gastroesophageal reflux disease and dysphagia. Endoscopy showed no esophageal stricture or Barrett esophagus. No evidence of gastric ulcer. Denies nausea, vomiting, or abdominal pain. Negative for H. pylori. Currently on pantoprazole 40mg twice daily and famotidine 20mg at bedtime. BMI: 32.4 kg/m2.

notes.description: Asthma exacerbation with respiratory medications
notes.text: Assessment: 32-year-old female with moderate persistent asthma presenting with acute exacerbation. SpO2: 94% on room air. Respiratory rate: 22/min. Heart rate: 98 bpm. No pneumonia on chest X-ray. Denies fever. Started on albuterol nebulizer and ipratropium. Continued on fluticasone inhaler and montelukast 10mg daily. No evidence of pneumothorax.

notes.description: Thyroid disorder with medication management
notes.text: Assessment: 55-year-old female with hypothyroidism on levothyroxine 100mcg daily. Labs show TSH normal. No symptoms of hyperthyroidism. Denies palpitations, tremor, or weight loss. Also has osteoporosis, taking alendronate 70mg weekly and calcium supplementation. BMI: 28.1 kg/m2. Blood pressure: 124/78 mmHg. Family history of mother with hypothyroidism.

notes.description: Deep vein thrombosis with anticoagulation
notes.text: Assessment: 49-year-old male with acute deep vein thrombosis of the left lower extremity. No evidence of pulmonary embolism on CT angiography. Denies chest pain, shortness of breath. Heart rate: 82 bpm. Blood pressure: 130/82 mmHg. SpO2: 99%. Started on rivaroxaban 15mg twice daily. Creatinine: 0.9 mg/dL. No history of prior DVT or PE.

notes.description: Urinary tract infection with antibiotic therapy
notes.text: Assessment: 35-year-old female with urinary tract infection. Denies fever or flank pain. No evidence of pyelonephritis. Started on trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole for 3 days. Allergic to penicillin. Currently takes sertraline 50mg for anxiety. Temperature: 37.0 C. Heart rate: 74 bpm. Blood pressure: 116/72 mmHg. WBC: 9.8 K/uL. Creatinine: 0.6 mg/dL.

notes.description: Abbreviation-heavy cardiology note
notes.text: Assessment: 73-year-old male with CHF, AFib, HTN, CKD stage 3, and hyperlipidemia. EF: 25%. BNP: 890 pg/mL. Currently on metoprolol, lisinopril, furosemide, apixaban, and atorvastatin. Denies chest pain or dyspnea. HR: 68 bpm. BP: 118/72 mmHg. Creatinine: 1.8 mg/dL. Potassium: 4.6 mmol/L.

notes.description: Uncertainty and differential diagnosis note
notes.text: Assessment: 40-year-old female presents with chest pain. Differential diagnosis includes costochondritis, gastroesophageal reflux disease, and possible pulmonary embolism. Cannot rule out acute coronary syndrome. Suspected anxiety contributing to symptoms. Troponin: <0.01 ng/mL. D-dimer pending. Blood pressure: 122/78 mmHg. Heart rate: 88 bpm. Started on pantoprazole 40mg.

notes.description: Comprehensive progress note with mixed assertions
notes.text: Progress Note: 63-year-old male with diabetes mellitus, hypertension, coronary artery disease status post stent placement, and benign prostatic hyperplasia. Hemoglobin: 14.2 g/dL. HbA1c: 6.9%. Blood pressure: 134/82 mmHg. Creatinine: 1.1 mg/dL. LDL: 78 mg/dL. Medications: metformin 1000mg, lisinopril 20mg, aspirin 81mg, atorvastatin 80mg, tamsulosin 0.4mg. No chest pain. Denies edema. Family history of father with diabetes mellitus.
